fields.name: config
documentation: Configure diagnostic options globally or for a specific diagnostic
Configuration can be specified globally, per-namespace, or ephemerally
(i.e. only for a single call to |vim.diagnostic.set()| or
followed by namespace configuration, and finally global configuration.
For example, if a user enables virtual text globally with
and a diagnostic producer sets diagnostics with
then virtual text will not be enabled for those diagnostics.
@*param* `opts` — When omitted or `nil`, retrieve the current
@*param* `namespace` — Update the options for the given namespace.
When omitted, update the global diagnostic options.
@*return* — : Current diagnostic config if {opts} is omitted.

fields.name: count
documentation: @*param* `bufnr` — Buffer number to get diagnostics from. Use 0 for
current buffer or nil for all buffers.
@*return* — : Table with actually present severity values as keys

fields.name: enable
documentation: @*param* `enable` — true/nil to enable, false to disable

fields.name: fromqflist
documentation: Convert a list of quickfix items to a list of diagnostics.
@*param* `list` — List of quickfix items from |getqflist()| or |getloclist()|.

fields.name: get
documentation: Modifying diagnostics in the returned table has no effect.
To set diagnostics in a buffer, use |vim.diagnostic.set()|.
@*param* `bufnr` — Buffer number to get diagnostics from. Use 0 for
current buffer or nil for all buffers.

fields.name: get_next
documentation: Get the next diagnostic closest to the cursor position.

fields.name: get_next_pos
documentation: Return the position of the next diagnostic in the current buffer.
@*return* — : Next diagnostic position as a `(row, col)` tuple or false if no next

fields.name: get_prev
documentation: Get the previous diagnostic closest to the cursor position.

fields.name: get_prev_pos
documentation: Return the position of the previous diagnostic in the current buffer.
@*return* — : Previous diagnostic position as a `(row, col)` tuple
or `false` if there is no prior diagnostic.

fields.name: goto_prev
documentation: Move to the previous diagnostic in the current buffer.

fields.name: hide
documentation: This only clears the decorations displayed in the buffer. Diagnostics can
be redisplayed with |vim.diagnostic.show()|. To completely remove
To hide diagnostics and prevent them from re-displaying, use
@*param* `bufnr` — Buffer number, or 0 for current buffer. When
omitted, hide diagnostics in all buffers.

fields.name: jump
documentation: @*return* — The diagnostic that was moved to.

fields.name: match
documentation: function M.match(str: string, pat: string, groups: string[], severity_map: table, defaults?: table)
Parse a diagnostic from a string.
For example, consider a line of output from a linter:
WARNING filename:27:3: Variable 'foo' does not exist
This can be parsed into |vim.Diagnostic| structure with:
local s = "WARNING filename:27:3: Variable 'foo' does not exist"
@*param* `groups` — List of fields in a |vim.Diagnostic| structure to
@*param* `severity_map` — A table mapping the severity field from {groups}
@*param* `defaults` — Table of default values for any fields not listed in {groups}.
When omitted, numeric values default to 0 and "severity" defaults to
@*return* — : |vim.Diagnostic| structure or `nil` if {pat} fails to match {str}.

fields.name: open_float
documentation: Show diagnostics in a floating window.

fields.name: reset
documentation: Remove all diagnostics from the given namespace.
Unlike |vim.diagnostic.hide()|, this function removes all saved
diagnostics. They cannot be redisplayed using |vim.diagnostic.show()|. To
simply remove diagnostic decorations in a way that they can be
@*param* `bufnr` — Remove diagnostics for the given buffer. When omitted,
diagnostics are removed for all buffers.

fields.name: set
documentation: function M.set(namespace: integer, bufnr: integer, diagnostics: vim.Diagnostic[], opts?: vim.diagnostic.Opts)
Set diagnostics for the given namespace and buffer.

fields.name: setloclist
documentation: Add buffer diagnostics to the location list.

fields.name: setqflist
documentation: Add all diagnostics to the quickfix list.

fields.name: show
documentation: function M.show(namespace?: integer, bufnr?: integer, diagnostics?: vim.Diagnostic[], opts?: vim.diagnostic.Opts)
Display diagnostics for the given namespace and buffer.
@*param* `bufnr` — Buffer number, or 0 for current buffer. When omitted, show
@*param* `diagnostics` — The diagnostics to display. When omitted, use the
saved diagnostics for the given namespace and
buffer. This can be used to display a list of diagnostics
without saving them or to display only a subset of
diagnostics. May not be used when {namespace}
